title: CLI Resilience: Timeout, Retry, and Error Classification
description: Walkthrough of CLI changes to add request timeout, retry with exponential backoff, HTML error detection, and proper error propagation.

steps.title: Retry and timeout constants
steps.description: MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, BASE_DELAY_MS=2000, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000, RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES=[429,500,502,503,504]. These match the server's retry pattern and add 500 to the retryable set since the server now returns 500 for external failures.

steps.title: HTML error response detection
steps.description: buildErrorMessage checks content-type for text/html and returns a clear message instead of dumping raw HTML. For non-HTML responses, includes the first 500 chars of the body.

steps.title: Main retry loop with timeout
steps.description: Each fetch call uses AbortSignal.timeout(30_000). On retryable responses (429/5xx), sleeps with exponential backoff before retrying. On network errors (TypeError, TimeoutError), also retries. Non-retryable errors (4xx) fail immediately.

steps.title: 429 Retry-After handling
steps.description: On HTTP 429, checks the Retry-After header. If present, uses that as the sleep duration (capped at 60s). Otherwise falls back to exponential backoff.

steps.title: Error wrapping for network failures
steps.description: listRepos call wrapped in try/catch. Network errors get a descriptive prefix ('Failed to fetch repository list: ...'). The repo-not-found process.exit(1) at line 36 is intentionally kept — it's a usage error, not a transient failure.

steps.title: Top-level error handler
steps.description: The command switch is wrapped in try/catch. Catches all errors thrown by command handlers (including the new throwing hxFetch), prints a clean error message to stderr, and exits with code 1. This replaces the scattered process.exit(1) calls.
